summary: A low rectangular single-building structure with a central raised curved dark roof and stepped terraces at the corners. The walls are horizontally banded with alternating materials and framed by white corner/edge blocks; a dotted white low boundary surrounds the footprint. The roof is made of a dark reddish material forming an asymmetric curved/hipped shape with small glass windows and a small flat top platform with glowstone.

shape.overall_form: rectangular base with a large curved/arched roof running along the long axis and stepped corner terraces
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along long axis with small asymmetries

materials.name: glass
materials.role: small windows in roof and walls

elements: banded walls with alternating brick, wood, and stone layers
white quartz corner/edge pillars and trim
large curved nether_brick roof with stepped slope and small glass panes
low dotted white boundary around building (single-block spaced)
small rooftop flat platform with glowstone
entrance area indicated by recessed section and stair-like light wood patch (uncertain exact door placement)
rebuild_hints: Start with a rectangular base roughly 13x25 blocks and raise walls ~4-5 blocks high with horizontal bands: bottom stonebrick strip, then alternating brick and wood plank layers separated by thin stone/quartz lines.
Add white quartz pillar/frame blocks at all corners and at mid-wall vertical frames; place a one-block-high quartz coping around the top of the walls.
Construct a curved roof of nether_brick along the long axis: build stepped layers that taper inward toward a small flat top platform; embed small glass panes symmetrically in the roof slopes.
Place a small secondary curved roof/annex on one short side (visible small half-dome), and add the dotted white boundary by placing single quartz blocks spaced one block apart around the perimeter.
Include small foundation/step details of stonebrick at ground level and a few gold/wood details near the recessed entrance area (exact door position uncertain).
uncertainties: Exact internal layout and whether there is a hidden second floor — exterior suggests single floor only.
Precise block types for the light brown horizontal band (identified as wood plank but could be stripped wood or another variant).
Exact footprint dimensions: estimated from aerial views; margin of error ±2 blocks in each axis.
Location and design of main entrance: recessed area with lighter blocks suggests entrance but door not clearly visible.
Some small decorative blocks (possible buttons, slabs, fences) are uncertain in type and placement.
